notes: SAP GUI selectors use automationid with technical path notation: /app/con[N]/ses[N]/wnd[N]/...
con[N] = connection index, ses[N] = session index, wnd[N] = window index (0 = main, 1+ = popups).
tbar[0] = main toolbar, tbar[1] = application toolbar.
usr/ = user area (main content), titl/ = titlebar area.
Field technical names follow SAP naming: program-fieldname (e.g., RSYST-BNAME).
Always use the UiPath SAP integration plugin for best reliability.
SAP Scripting must be enabled: RZ11 -> sapgui/user_scripting = TRUE.
Session indices may vary if multiple SAP sessions are open -- parameterize con and ses indices.

transaction_code_field.description: Transaction code input field (command bar at top of SAP window). Type /nXXXX and press Enter to navigate.

enter_button.description: Enter/Continue button on the main toolbar (green checkmark)

save_button.description: Save button (floppy disk icon) on the main toolbar (Ctrl+S)

back_button.description: Back button (green arrow) on the main toolbar (F3)

exit_button.description: Exit/Cancel button (red X) on the main toolbar (Shift+F3)

cancel_button.description: Cancel button on the main toolbar (F12)

username_field.description: Username input field on SAP logon screen

password_field.description: Password input field on SAP logon screen

client_field.description: Client/Mandant number input field on SAP logon screen

language_field.description: Language input field on SAP logon screen

text_input_field.description: Generic text input field parameterized by SAP technical field name (e.g., VBAK-VBELN)

combobox_field.description: Dropdown/combobox field in the user area

table_cell_text.description: Display-only text cell in a SAP table control. col and row are 0-based.

table_cell_input.description: Editable input cell in a SAP table control. col and row are 0-based.

alv_grid_shell.description: ALV Grid control (shell container). Use GetCellValue/SetCellValue for cell access.
alv_grid_shell.notes: For ALV grids, interact via the shell object methods rather than individual cell selectors.

status_bar.description: Status bar at the bottom of the SAP window. Read text to detect success/error messages.

popup_dialog_continue.description: Continue/Enter button on a SAP popup dialog (wnd[1] = first popup)

app_toolbar_button.description: Button on the application toolbar (tbar[1]). Index is 0-based, varies by transaction.
